chapter_title.en: Chapter on the mutamatti‘ Leaving Mecca and Returning

text: 2752 - قَالَ اَلصَّادِقُ عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ : «إِذَا أَرَادَ اَلْمُتَمَتِّعُ اَلْخُرُوجَ مِنْ مَكَّةَ إِلَى بَعْضِ اَلْمَوَاضِعِ فَلَيْسَ لَهُ ذَلِكَ لِأَنَّهُ مُرْتَبِطٌ بِالْحَجِّ حَتَّى يَقْضِيَهُ إِلاَّ أَنْ يَعْلَمَ أَنَّهُ لاَ يَفُوتُهُ اَلْحَجُّ فَإِذَا عَلِمَ وَ خَرَجَ وَ عَادَ فِي اَلشَّهْرِ اَلَّذِي خَرَجَ فِيهِ دَخَلَ مَكَّةَ مُحِلاًّ وَ إِنْ دَخَلَهَا فِي غَيْرِ ذَلِكَ اَلشَّهْرِ دَخَلَهَا مُحْرِماً ».

en.bab-ul-qaim-publications: Hadith.2752 - Imam Jafar ibn Muhammad Al-Sadiq (as) said: "If a person performing tamattu‘ wishes to leave Mecca for another place, they are not permitted to do so because they are bound to Hajj until they complete it, unless they are certain that they will not miss the Hajj. If they are certain and leave but return within the same month in which they departed, they may enter Mecca in the state of non-ihram. However, if they return in a different month, they must enter Mecca in the state of ihram."
